raw_consumer_statement:
Updated CFPB Complaint Reopening/ Incomplete Resolution Original Complaint ID : XXXX was not resolved Fully Dear CFPB, I am writing to reopen and supplement my original complaint regarding American Expresss handling of XXXX related disputes concerning the same merchant : XXXX XXXX XXXX 

There were XXXX charges/disputes involved, both filed on XX/XX/year> Dispute XXXX : {$100.00} Deposit Dispute XXXX : {$5400.00} Program Balance ( re-Opened on XX/XX/XXXX ) Total : {$5500.00} Both disputes were initially denied by Amex due to being past 60 days, even though I submitted documentation explaining that the harm was not discoverable until after that period due to merchant deception and a third-party whistleblower. 

What happened next : On XX/XX/year>, I recieved an email from Amex that they are to credit my account for the {$100.00} deposit ( XXXX ) without reopening the dispute or asking for further information. However, as of XX/XX/XXXX, this credit still does not appear on my account. 

For the {$5400.00} balance ( XXXX ), I reopened the dispute on XX/XX/XXXX. On XX/XX/XXXX, Amex closed the case again, now citing a tort claim exclusion under consumer credit law and stating they can not process the claim further. 

Why this response is inconsistent and unreasonable : Both charges are for the exact same service and merchant. If the {$100.00} was refunded, it logically follows that the $ XXXX of the same transactionshould be treated under the same standard. 

American Express has not explained why the lower-dollar dispute was credited while the higher-value dispute was denied under an unrelated legal theory. 

I am not pursuing a tort claimI am seeking relief for a misrepresented and deceptively marketed service, which is absolutely within the scope of credit dispute protections. 

I respectfully request that CFPB investigate : Why Amex inconsistently applied different legal standards to the same dispute. 

Why the {$100.00} credit has not yet posted, despite written confirmation. 

Why a documented pattern of merchant misrepresentation is being dismissed under a tort claim label without proper justification. 

Thank you for your continued attention to this matter. I am happy to resubmit all supporting documentation if needed. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX
